name: Piyush Rajendra Ladukar
title: Backend Developer | Python | Machine Learning | Building in GenAI & Agentic AI
summary: Final-year B.Tech CSE student with hands-on experience in backend development, building secure REST APIs, and developing scalable, data-driven applications using real-world datasets. Passionate about AI and currently building LLM-powered applications while expanding expertise in Generative AI, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and AI agents. Strong foundation in Python, backend systems, and problem-solving, with a proven track record of delivering end-to-end projects. Seeking Backend Developer, Python Developer, or AI/ML Engineer internships and full-time opportunities.

bullets: Built and maintained 12 REST API endpoints using Node.js, Express.js, and PostgreSQL for internal enterprise applications supporting CIRP/IBC advisory workflows.
Implemented JWT Authentication and RBAC-based authorization systems, improving secure access control and backend security workflows.
Collaborated on secure enterprise systems integrating authentication, role management, and database-driven services.

projects.name: SecondBrain
projects.tagline: Personal AI Agent & RAG System (In Development)
bullets: Building a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline with LangChain and a vector database to power a personal knowledge-agent system with LLM-based reasoning and tool calling.
Designing agent memory and multi-step workflow logic for context retrieval, embeddings generation, and structured LLM responses.

projects.name: LokDrishti
bullets: Built a full-stack civic data platform scraping and structuring Lok Sabha MP data (attendance, debates, criminal records) for 543 MPs; deployed Flask APIs and a React dashboard via Supabase with OAuth.

projects.name: AatankDrishti
bullets: Engineered a data pipeline and React dashboard analyzing 181K+ terrorism incidents across 200+ countries from the GTD dataset.

projects.name: Why Farmer Suicides
bullets: Analyzed Maharashtra farmer suicide data (2014-2023) across 33 districts, building dashboards correlating debt, crop failure, and weather patterns.

projects.name: Anomax
bullets: Developed a machine learning model to detect anomalous electricity consumption patterns; published findings in the International Journal of Versatile Research and Analysis (IJVRA).
